data.title: Shanghai Conant Optical Unveils HK$400 Million H-Share Buyback Plan for 2026
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shanghai Conant Optical Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2276) ) has issued an update.

Shanghai Conant Optical Co., Ltd. has approved a 2026 H-share repurchase plan under an existing and a proposed new repurchase mandate, signaling confidence in its future prospects and valuation. The initiative aims to protect shareholder interests and bolster investor confidence by buying back shares on the Hong Kong market.

The company plans to allocate up to HK$400 million, funded mainly from internal cash and other permitted sources, to repurchase no more than 10% of its issued H-shares under the current mandate, with a potential total not expected to exceed 20,230,616 shares. Repurchase prices will comply with Hong Kong listing rules, and the board has committed not to reduce the public float below regulatory thresholds or trigger a mandatory general offer, limiting structural impact on existing shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2276) stock is a Buy with a HK$69.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shanghai Conant Optical Co. Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2276 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shanghai Conant Optical Co. Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Conant Optical Co., Ltd. is a China-based joint stock company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the optical industry. The group focuses on the production and sale of optical products, particularly H-shares listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, targeting investors in the Hong Kong and broader international capital markets.

Average Trading Volume: 1,940,561

data.title: Bellevue Gold posts record cash flow as grades rise and hedge book cut
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Bellevue Gold Limited ( (AU:BGL) ).

Bellevue Gold reported March quarter production of 40,745 ounces of gold at an improved head grade of 4.7 grams per tonne, with metallurgical recoveries averaging 94.6%, keeping the company on track to meet its FY26 production and cost guidance. The miner continued to benefit from its high renewable energy penetration of about 90%, which helped keep diesel costs among the lowest in the sector.

Stronger grades and operational performance drove record underlying free cash flow of about $158 million for the quarter, lifting cash and gold on hand to approximately $181 million. Bellevue also accelerated pre-deliveries into its forward gold sales, cutting hedge commitments by about 26% to 91,650 ounces and leaving it free of contractual hedge deliveries until the end of 2026, a move designed to de-risk the balance sheet while increasing future exposure to spot gold prices and supporting exploration and growth options.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:BGL) stock is a Buy with a A$2.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bellevue Gold Limited stock, see the AU:BGL Stock Forecast page.

More about Bellevue Gold Limited

Bellevue Gold Limited is an Australian gold mining company listed on the ASX, focused on developing and operating its Bellevue underground gold mine in Western Australia. The company targets efficient, high-grade gold production with a strong emphasis on renewable energy use to lower costs and reduce exposure to diesel price volatility.

Average Trading Volume: 10,334,998

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$2.28B

data.title: Stryve Foods Delays Annual 10-K Yearly Report
data.description:
Stryve Foods ( (SNAX) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Stryve Foods, Inc. has notified investors that it will be late filing its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, via a Form 12b-25. The delayed filing concerns the company’s annual Form 10-K (Yearly Report), not any quarterly or foreign issuer reports.

The company cites working capital constraints as the main reason for the delay in compiling, disseminating and reviewing the information required for the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). These cash and resource pressures have made a timely filing impracticable without what Stryve calls undue hardship and expense.

Because of these constraints, Stryve’s independent registered public accounting firm has not yet completed its audit procedures on the 2025 financial statements. The audit delay directly contributes to the company’s inability to meet the original Form 10-K (Yearly Report) deadline.

Stryve states that it expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on or before the 15th calendar day following the prescribed due date, in line with the relief allowed under Form 12b-25. The company also notes it is taking responsibility to submit the report as soon as practical, signaling an intention not to run the full extension if avoidable.

Management does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year based on information currently available. Nevertheless, investors should treat these comments as forward-looking, as final numbers and disclosures could still change once the audit is completed and the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed.

Stryve affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, suggesting the delay is isolated to this annual report. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Financial Officer Carolyn Short on April 7, 2026, underscoring ongoing efforts to remain in compliance while managing current financial and audit constraints.

Spark’s Take on SNAX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SNAX is a Underperform.

Stryve Foods’ overall stock score reflects significant financial challenges, including negative cash flows and high leverage, which weigh heavily on the company’s stability and growth prospects. Despite improvements in operational efficiency and strategic partnerships, ongoing financial constraints and bearish technical indicators contribute to a low overall score. The company needs to address its financial health to improve investor confidence and capitalize on growth opportunities.

To see Spark’s full report on SNAX stock, click here.

More about Stryve Foods

Average Trading Volume: 3,402

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $56.44K

Alchemy Investments Acquisition Corp 1 Class A ( (ALCY) ) has shared an update.

On April 7, 2026, Alchemy Investments Acquisition Corp 1 and Cartiga, LLC released an April 2026 investor presentation outlining details of a potential business combination between the SPAC and Cartiga. The materials are intended to support investor outreach around the deal and will be accompanied by SEC filings, including a planned Form S-4 registration statement with proxy and prospectus materials for Alchemy shareholders.

The presentation underscores that Alchemy, Cartiga and their respective leadership teams may be considered participants in the proxy solicitation related to the proposed transaction. It also highlights extensive risk disclosures, emphasizing that completion of the business combination is subject to shareholder approvals, regulatory clearances, market and economic conditions, and other uncertainties that could materially affect the outcome and future performance of the combined company.

More about Alchemy Investments Acquisition Corp 1 Class A

Alchemy Investments Acquisition Corp 1 is a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) formed to pursue a business combination, and operates through Alchemy Acquisition Holdings, Inc. as its holding company. The vehicle focuses on identifying and merging with target businesses, providing them access to public capital markets, and is currently concentrating on a potential combination with legal-asset-focused firm Cartiga, LLC.

Ellington Financial ( (EFC) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 7, 2026, Ellington Financial Inc. announced that its board declared a monthly common stock dividend of $0.13 per share, underscoring the company’s continued emphasis on distributing regular income to shareholders. The dividend will be paid on May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 30, 2026, offering income-focused investors greater visibility into near-term cash returns.

The declaration reinforces Ellington Financial’s role as an income-oriented vehicle in the mortgage and structured credit space, where stable dividends are a key part of the investor value proposition. data.title: Portofino Resources to Consolidate Shares 10-for-1 to Bolster Financing Flexibility
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Portofino Resources ( (TSE:POR) ).

Portofino Resources Inc. has approved a 10-for-1 share consolidation that will reduce its outstanding common shares from about 175.4 million to approximately 17.5 million, with all warrants and stock options consolidated on the same basis. The company expects the move to improve its ability to obtain less dilutive financing while maintaining its name and stock symbol, though shareholders with physical certificates will need to exchange them once the consolidation is cleared by the TSX Venture Exchange, with fractional shares rounded down.

The consolidation is intended to support Portofino’s broader exploration and project expansion plans across its lithium and gold assets in Argentina and Canada. By tightening its capital structure without requiring shareholder approval, the board aims to enhance the company’s financial flexibility and its positioning within the junior resource exploration sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:POR) stock is a Sell with a C$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Portofino Resources stock, see the TSE:POR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on POR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, POR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (no revenue, negative equity, and ongoing cash burn), despite improved 2025 loss and cash usage. Technical signals are mixed-to-weak with a flat price profile and slightly negative MACD. The warrant extension is a modest positive for financing flexibility, while valuation cannot be meaningfully assessed due to missing P/E and dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on POR stock, click here.

More about Portofino Resources

Portofino Resources Inc. is a Vancouver-based mineral exploration company focused on projects in the Americas. It holds a 100% interest in the drill-ready Yergo Lithium Project in Catamarca, Argentina, within the Argentine Lithium Triangle, and two gold exploration projects in northwestern Ontario, including the South of Otter, Red Lake project and the Gold Creek, Thunder Bay project, the latter optioned to Delta Resources Limited.

Obsidian Energy ( (TSE:OBE) ) has provided an announcement.

Obsidian Energy has called its 2026 annual and special meeting of shareholders for May 7, 2026, in Calgary, issuing a management information circular and proxy materials dated March 15, 2026. Shareholders will vote on routine annual matters, including receiving 2025 financial statements, appointing auditors and electing directors, as well as several compensation and equity-related items.

The agenda includes an advisory vote on the company’s executive pay practices, amendments to its stock option plan, and approval of all unallocated options and share unit awards under its equity incentive plans. The meeting will help shape Obsidian’s governance and compensation framework for the coming year, with both registered and beneficial shareholders instructed to submit proxies or voting instructions ahead of the May 5, 2026 deadline.
